title: 2025-26 EFL League One - Managerial changes
text: The 2025-26 EFL League One season witnessed significant managerial upheaval both before and during the campaign. Several notable changes occurred in the pre-season period as newly-relegated clubs restructured their coaching setups. Pre-Season Appointments: Cardiff City appointed Brian Barry-Murphy on June 16, 2025, replacing interim manager Aaron Ramsey following the club's Championship relegation. Huddersfield Town brought in Lee Grant on May 28, 2025, after Jon Worthington's interim spell concluded. Plymouth Argyle installed Tom Cleverley as their new manager on June 13, 2025, following Miron Muslic's departure to FC Schalke 04. In-Season Changes: The campaign saw widespread managerial turbulence. Mike Dodds became the first casualty when Wycombe Wanderers sacked him on September 18, 2025, with the club sitting in 19th place. Michael Duff replaced him the same day. Steve Bruce departed Blackpool on October 4, 2025, with Ian Evatt taking over on October 21. Luton Town sacked Matt Bloomfield on October 6, 2025, appointing Jack Wilshere five days later. Peterborough United parted ways with Darren Ferguson on October 25, 2025, appointing Luke Williams on October 29. Reading dismissed Noel Hunt on October 26, 2025, replacing him with Leam Richardson on October 28. Port Vale's Darren Moore left on December 28, 2025, with Jon Brady appointed on January 6, 2026. Huddersfield Town sacked Lee Grant on January 17, 2026, bringing in Liam Manning on January 20. Wigan Athletic dismissed Ryan Lowe on February 8, 2026, appointing Gary Caldwell on February 16. Exeter City subsequently lost Caldwell to Wigan on the same date, promoting Matt Taylor on March 3. Kevin Nolan's departure from Northampton Town came on March 9, 2026, with Lee Clark appointed to Rotherham United on March 18. Lincoln City won the league with 103 points under Michael Skubala, while Cardiff City secured automatic promotion with 91 points. The playoff positions saw Stockport County, Bradford City, Bolton Wanderers, and Stevenage competing for the third promotion spot, ultimately won by Bolton Wanderers following their victory over Stockport County in the playoff final.
